# Filter

Provides compact frontend controls for narrowing a collection or search result.

## Contents

- [Installation](#installation)
- [Usage](#usage)
- [Available controls](#available-controls)
- [Controlled values](#controlled-values)
- [Design and accessibility guidance](#design-and-accessibility-guidance)

## Installation

Add the Filter components from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/filter
```

## Usage

Compose only the controls that match the collection's useful facets, then bind each one to frontend state. Keep filters together in a wrapping toolbar, use domain-specific titles, and show the current selections close to their triggers.

**Filter toolbar example**

```vue
<template>
  <div class="flex flex-wrap items-center gap-2">
    <FilterSearchInput v-model="search" aria-label="Search projects" />
    <FilterCheckbox v-model="active" title="Active only" />
    <FilterNumberInput v-model="amount" title="Amount" />
  </div>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { ref } from 'vue'
import {
  FilterCheckbox,
  FilterNumberInput,
  FilterSearchInput,
} from '@/Components/Filter'

const search = ref('')
const active = ref(false)
const amount = ref<string | null>(null)
</script>
```

## Available controls

### Search

`FilterSearchInput` binds a string or number model and uses the local Input's 300 ms debounce. Add an accessible label that names the searched collection; the built-in “Search…” placeholder is not a replacement for a label.

```vue
<FilterSearchInput v-model="search" aria-label="Search projects" />
```

### Boolean filter

`FilterCheckbox` toggles its model when the compact outline button is activated. Use it only for an independent boolean facet such as “Active only.” Its required `title` is the visible label; write the title as a condition rather than an ambiguous command.

### Multiple selection

`FilterMultiSelect` receives `options` with `label` and `value` fields and binds a `string[]`. Option values are normalized to strings. The trigger shows up to two selected labels on large screens and switches to a count for larger selections; the popover provides filtering and a clear action.

```vue
<FilterMultiSelect
  v-model="statuses"
  title="Status"
  :options="[
    { label: 'Draft', value: 'draft' },
    { label: 'Published', value: 'published' },
  ]"
/>
```

### Date range

Bind `v-model:from` and `v-model:until` to calendar date strings. The control emits each boundary separately and its Clear action returns both to `undefined`. The current implementation displays two months and uses Slovak calendar and formatting locales; treat that as a local constraint until locale props are added.

```vue
<FilterDateRange
  v-model:from="createdFrom"
  v-model:until="createdUntil"
  title="Created"
/>
```

### Numeric operators

`FilterNumberInput` binds a string or `null`. It encodes one-value comparisons as `operator:value`, for example `gte:10`, and ranges as `be:10:20` or `nbe:10:20`. Supported one-value operators are `lt`, `lte`, `eq`, `gte`, and `gt`. Invalid or incomplete input emits `null` when the popover closes.

### Reset

`FilterResetButton` accepts the frontend filter object. It is rendered only while `filter.applied` is true and calls `filter.reset()` when activated. Place it after the facet controls so clearing the complete filter state is easy to find.

## Controlled values

These controls edit local values; they do not decide how a collection loads or how state is serialized outside the component. Keep one source of truth, pass updated external values back into the controls, and use the encoded numeric and date formats exactly as documented above. Reset all related values together so the toolbar and displayed results cannot disagree.

## Design and accessibility guidance

Use filters for optional narrowing, not for required form answers. Preserve a visible result count or summary when a filter materially changes what is shown. Filter triggers must have specific visible titles, and icon-only clear actions need accessible names. After a filter changes results, keep focus on the control unless the user explicitly asked to move elsewhere.

> **Use form controls for submitted data: [Field](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/field)**
>
> For required values, validation, descriptions, and errors that belong to a form submission, compose regular controls inside Field instead of using the compact Filter toolbar controls.

Hosted documentation: [Filter](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/filter)
